mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or signal fails to predict direction in backtests with p < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based domestic demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices indicate strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate earnings and positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in >55% of observations during recovery phases, or predictive alpha decays to zero within 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and consumer spending power, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll increases of any magnitude fail to predict positive 5-day returns, with directional accuracy below 50% across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation spikes above 0.4% force expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL thresholds above 0.4% are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in >60% of instances, or inflation signal loses predictive power after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP growth and monthly consumption expenditure expansion validate sustained aggregate demand, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and positive 20-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Combined GDP and consumption signals yield negative 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a manufacturing contraction phase signals weakening supply-side momentum, triggering immediate risk-off flows and negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during contraction phases are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in >60% of cases, or the signal shows no directional edge in walk-forward validation.
